package transport

import (
	"net/http"

	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
)

// Positive scenario for response_dto_contains_gorm_tags.
// Anti-pattern: Response DTO carries gorm persistence tags,
// coupling the API response shape to the database schema.

type OrderResponse struct {
	ID         uint    `json:"id" gorm:"column:id;primaryKey"`
	CustomerID uint    `json:"customer_id" gorm:"column:customer_id;index"`
	ProductID  uint    `json:"product_id" gorm:"column:product_id"`
	Quantity   int     `json:"quantity" gorm:"column:quantity"`
	Status     string  `json:"status" gorm:"column:status"`
	TotalPrice float64 `json:"total_price" gorm:"column:total_price"`
	CreatedAt  string  `json:"created_at" gorm:"column:created_at"`
	UpdatedAt  string  `json:"updated_at" gorm:"column:updated_at"`
}

func GetOrderHandler(c *gin.Context) {
	var order OrderResponse
	if err := db.Where("id = ?", c.Param("id")).First(&order).Error; err != nil {
		c.JSON(http.StatusNotFound, gin.H{"error": "order not found"})
		return
	}

	// Anti-pattern: the response DTO has gorm tags, leaking persistence
	// concerns into the API response layer.
	c.JSON(http.StatusOK, order)
}
